description: This scanning electron micrograph presents three surface views of Fe-Mn-Al alloys after isothermal oxidation at 800°C for 24 hr in pure oxygen at 200 torr, specifically illustrating areas of spalled alumina and void formation. Subfigure (a) shows Fe-25Mn-8Al exhibiting angular voids beneath spalled regions, characteristic of region II duplex alloys where austenite promotes alumina scale breakdown. Subfigure (b) depicts Fe-30Mn-9Al with smooth, rounded voids, which the text notes form on all alloys containing more than 25% manganese regardless of aluminum concentration. Subfigure (c) reveals Fe-30Mn-12Al where numerous smooth rounded voids appear filled or partially filled with manganese oxide nodules. Together, these micrographs demonstrate a compositional transition in void morphology from angular to rounded as manganese content increases above 25%, and provide direct visual evidence supporting the proposed mechanism of manganese oxide nodule nucleation and growth via manganese vapor reaction within voids beneath the alumina scale.
